compatibilityLabel: React DOM 19.2.6 root and portal listener installation oracle

generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree and probed through a deterministic minimal DOM host
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per React DOM listener-installation scenario and mode
generation.pathNormalization: temporary extraction paths and React randomized listener marker names are not serialized

probeModes.reason: Default Node development probes createRoot, hydrateRoot, and portal listener installation with development diagnostics enabled.
Default Node production probes the same createRoot, hydrateRoot, and portal listener installation behavior against the production React DOM client bundle.

scenarios.expectedBehavior: createRoot installs one all-supported-event listener set on the root container and one selectionchange listener on the owner document.
Calling createRoot twice for the same container does not install duplicate root-container or owner-document listeners.
hydrateRoot installs one all-supported-event listener set on the root container and one selectionchange listener on the owner document.
Calling hydrateRoot twice for the same container does not install duplicate root-container or owner-document listeners.
Calling hydrateRoot after createRoot on the same container reuses the existing listener set without duplicate registration.
Two roots in the same owner document each get a root listener set, while owner-document selectionchange remains deduped.
Rendering a portal installs the same all-supported-event listener set on the portal container without duplicating owner-document selectionchange.
Rendering a portal into a different owner document installs portal-container listeners and selectionchange on that portal owner document.

delegatedEventExamples.reason: Click is a canonical delegated root event with both capture and bubble listeners.
Mouse move is a delegated continuous event with capture and bubble root listeners.
Wheel is delegated and uses passive listener options when passive support is detected.
Touch start is delegated and uses passive listener options when passive support is detected.
Touch move is delegated and uses passive listener options when passive support is detected.

selectionEvent.reason: React DOM installs selectionchange on the owner document rather than the root or portal container.

args.value: You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot() before. Instead, call root.render() on the existing root instead if you want to update it.
You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot() before. Instead, call root.render() on the existing root instead if you want to update it.
You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot() before. Instead, call root.render() on the existing root instead if you want to update it.

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM 19.2.6 behavior only; Fast React DOM listener implementation is outside this worker scope.

The oracle intentionally stops at root and portal listener installation side effects. Synthetic event extraction, dispatch order, batching, and prevent/propagation behavior are covered by separate event oracles.
Listener wrapper priority is not asserted here because worker 048 owns the React DOM event-priority oracle surface.
The deterministic host implements only the DOM APIs needed for createRoot, flushSync, and portal listener installation; browser matrix behavior belongs in later browser-backed event oracles.
